quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 3 Quiz 12
quiz.subtitle: Mixed Review of Parallel and Perpendicular Lines
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 3.11 Problem-Solving with Parallel and Perpendicular Lines

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, forming alternate interior angles of $(8x - 29)^\circ$ and $(5x + 13)^\circ$. What is the measure of the second angle?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are congruent when lines are parallel: $8x - 29 = 5x + 13$. Solving: $3x = 42$, so $x = 14$. Then $5(14) + 13 = 83^\circ$.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, forming same-side interior angles of $(6x + 17)^\circ$ and $(3x + 10)^\circ$. What is the measure of the smaller angle?
questions.explanation: Same-side interior angles are supplementary: $(6x + 17) + (3x + 10) = 180$. Simplifying: $9x + 27 = 180$, so $9x = 153$, giving $x = 17$. The angles are $119^\circ$ and $61^\circ$. The smaller is $61^\circ$.

questions.question: Two lines are cut by a transversal, forming corresponding angles of $(9x - 44)^\circ$ and $(6x - 14)^\circ$. What value of $x$ proves the lines are parallel?
questions.explanation: By the converse of the Corresponding Angles Postulate, the lines are parallel if the angles are congruent: $9x - 44 = 6x - 14$. Solving: $3x = 30$, giving $x = 10$.

questions.question: A line passes through $(-2, 9)$ and $(4, -15)$. What is its slope?
A line passes through $(6, -1)$ with a slope of $-3$. What is its $y$-intercept?
questions.explanation: Using point-slope form: $y - (-1) = -3(x - 6)$, so $y + 1 = -3x + 18$, giving $y = -3x + 17$. The $y$-intercept is $17$.

questions.question: Line $m$ passes through $(3, -5)$ and $(7, 7)$. Line $n$ is parallel to line $m$. What is the slope of line $n$?
questions.explanation: First find the slope of line $m$: $m = \frac{7 - (-5)}{7 - 3} = \frac{12}{4} = 3$. Parallel lines have equal slopes, so line $n$ also has a slope of $3$.

questions.question: Line $r$ has equation $4x - 20y = 60$. What is the slope of a line perpendicular to $r$?
questions.explanation: Convert to slope-intercept form: $-20y = -4x + 60$, so $y = \frac{1}{5}x - 3$. The slope of $r$ is $\frac{1}{5}$. A perpendicular line has slope $-5$.

questions.question: A line passes through $(8, -3)$ and is perpendicular to $y = 4x - 11$. What is its $y$-intercept?
questions.explanation: The given line has slope $4$, so a perpendicular line has slope $-\frac{1}{4}$. Using point-slope form: $y - (-3) = -\frac{1}{4}(x - 8)$, so $y + 3 = -\frac{1}{4}x + 2$, giving $y = -\frac{1}{4}x - 1$. The $y$-intercept is $-1$.

questions.question: Segment $AB$ has endpoints $A(-3, 2)$ and $B(1, 14)$. A second segment has slope $-\frac{1}{3}$. What is the relationship between the two segments?
questions.explanation: Slope of $AB = \frac{14 - 2}{1 - (-3)} = \frac{12}{4} = 3$. The second segment has slope $-\frac{1}{3}$. Since $3 \cdot \left(-\frac{1}{3}\right) = -1$, the segments are perpendicular.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, forming alternate exterior angles of $(7x - 19)^\circ$ and $(4x + 17)^\circ$. What is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: Alternate exterior angles are congruent when lines are parallel: $7x - 19 = 4x + 17$. Solving: $3x = 36$, giving $x = 12$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the full range of parallel and perpendicular line concepts including angle theorems, slope calculations, and writing equations.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the core concepts but review distinguishing between opposite slopes and negative reciprocal slopes when determining perpendicularity.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: corresponding, alternate interior, and alternate exterior angles are congruent; same-side interior angles are supplementary; parallel lines have equal slopes; perpendicular lines have negative reciprocal slopes (product is $-1$).
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Review the angle theorems for parallel lines, the slope formula $m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$, and how to write equations using point-slope form $y - y_1 = m(x - x_1)$.
